title: The Energy Risk Remains (Part I)
text:
The Energy Risk Remains (Part I)

Louis-Vincent Gave

Gavekal Research | 22 Jul 2026

Energy stocks, as a GICS sector, have delivered the best total return performance of any US sector over the past five years. Better yet, this performance has been broadly uncorrelated with almost all other asset sectors and asset classes. When stocks and bonds fared poorly in the wake of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, energy stocks soared. Energy stocks gapped higher again when the loss of shipping insurance effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz. Hindsight is, of course, 20/20, but the portfolio to have had over the past five years would have been 50% invested in US tech stocks and 50% invested in US energy stocks (in fairness, a sprinkle of gold and precious-metal miners would have also helped!). Such a portfolio would have delivered powerful and very steady returns…Given the above uncorrelated and very solid performance of energy stocks, one might think that energy names would be all the rage among investors today. But one would be wrong. In fact, while tech stocks now account for almost 40% of the S&P 500’s market capitalization, energy stocks, with their 3% weight, are close to record lows.
